zh.Drinkstone, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可德林可斯同
zh.lifeSummary: 當Baron John Bacon I於1272年出生在英國素福爾可德林可斯同時，他的父親Robert Bacon27歲，母親Alice Burgate35歲。他於1299年與Alice Assey在英國英格蘭素福爾可賀瑟特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1358年在英國素福爾可賀瑟特享年86歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可賀瑟特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Baron John Bacon I于1272年出生在英国素福爾可德林可斯同时，他的父亲Robert Bacon27岁，母亲Alice Burgate35岁。他于1299年与Alice Assey在英国英格兰素福爾可賀瑟特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1358年在英国素福爾可賀瑟特享年86岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可賀瑟特。

nameMeanings.name: Bacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Norman French personal name Bacun, derived from the ancient Germanic name Bac(c)o, Bahho, based on the element bag ‘(to) fight, (to) dispute’. The name was relatively common among the Normans in the form Bacus, of which the oblique case was Bacon.

English and French: from Middle English, Old French bacun, bacon ‘bacon’ (a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to Back 3), probably a metonymic occupational name for a preparer and seller of cured pork.

History: Gilles Bacon from Normandy, France, is documented in Quebec City, QC, in 1647. — Michael Bacon from England arrived in Dedham, MA, in 1640. Nathanial Bacon, from Stratton, Cornwall, arrived in Barnstaple, MA, in 1639. Another Nathaniel Bacon (1647–76), from Friston Hall, Suffolk, emigrated to VA and settled at Curl's Neck on the James river.

variants.name: Bagg
meanings: from Middle English bagge ‘bag, pack, bundle’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of bags and sacks of various kinds, including wallets and purses. It might also have been used for a beggar, or for one who carried their belongings in a bag.
from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago (Bac(c)o, Bahho), from the root bag- ‘to fight’ (see Bacon 2).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the title of nobility, along the lines of Earl and Duke . The title arose in the Norman feudal system and seems to be of Germanic origin, derived from Old English beorn ‘young warrior’ or a related continental Germanic form. In part the given name, and in particular the variant Barron , may represent the surname derived from the title.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Baron John Bacon I was born in 1272, in Drinkstone, Suffolk, England, his father, Robert Bacon, was 27 and his mother, Alice Burgate, was 35. He married Alice Assey in 1299, in Hessett, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1358, in Hessett, Suffolk, England, at the age of 86, and was buried in Hessett, Suffolk, England.

fr.Bradfield St George, Suffolk, England: Bradfield St George, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Baron John Bacon I est né en 1272, à Drinkstone, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Robert Bacon, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Alice Burgate, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Alice Assey en 1299, à Hessett, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1358, à Hessett, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 86 ans, et a été enterré à Hessett, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.Bradfield St George, Suffolk, England: Bradfield St George, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Baron John Bacon I 1272 in Drinkstone, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Bacon 27 und seine Mutter Alice Burgate 35. Er hat 1299 in Hessett, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Alice Assey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1358 im Alter von 86 Jahren in Hessett, Suffolk, England und wurde in Hessett, Suffolk, England begraben.

Lui sposato Alice Assey nel 1299, nel Hessett, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1358, nel Hessett, Suffolk, England, all'età di 86 anni, e fu sepolto a Hessett, Suffolk, England.

ko.Bradfield St George, Suffolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 브래드필드 스트 조지
ko.lifeSummary: Baron John Bacon I(이)가 1272년 영국 수폴크 드링크스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Bacon(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Burgate(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1299년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 헤싯에서 Alice Assey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1358년 영국 수폴크 헤싯에서 86세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 헤싯에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Baron John Bacon I родился в 1272 г. в местности дринкстоун, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Bacon было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Burgate, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Assey в 1299 г. в местности хессетт, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1358 г. в местности хессетт, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 86 лет и был похоронен в местности хессетт, суффолк, Англия.
